codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully adopts shared EmptyState, Skeleton loading, and shared Input/Select components across the admin pages. ChangelogPage now uses Skeleton blocks instead of a Loader2 spinner for loading state, and uses the shared EmptyState component for empty state. NotificationsPage uses shared Select and Input components. ApprovalsPage and AuditTrailPage (both tabs) use shared EmptyState. All changes are clean, consistent with the shared component APIs, and tests were updated accordingly.

issues.description: The Select component's 'label' prop renders a plain text label ('Recipient'), but the original inline label included a Users icon (<Users size={13} /> Recipient). The shared Select component's label prop is a string type and doesn't support icons. This is a minor visual regression where the icon is lost in the non-loading state. During loading, the skeleton still shows the icon label. The icon inconsistency between loading and loaded states is worth noting but not blocking since the Select component API doesn't support icon labels.

The textarea for 'Body' remains a native HTML element with inline styles rather than being converted to a shared component. However, no shared Textarea component exists in the component library, so this is acceptable. The focus styling was updated to match the shared Input/Select pattern (focus:border + focus:shadow-sm), which is good.

sharedStateObservations.observation: DataTable component uses a simple 'emptyMessage' string prop for its empty state, which renders a plain text message. This means pages using DataTable for empty states (UsersPage, RolesPage) cannot use the richer EmptyState component with icon, title, description, and action props. This is why those pages were not modified in this feature — their empty states are handled by DataTable internally.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/ui/DataTable.tsx:211-217 renders emptyMessage as plain text in a <td>. UsersPage.tsx:912 uses emptyMessage='No users yet...', RolesPage.tsx:302 uses emptyMessage='No roles configured yet'. The feature description listed UsersPage and RolesPage but they were correctly skipped since DataTable handles their empty states.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared Select and Input components don't support a 'leftIcon' or icon in the label. The original NotificationsPage Recipient label had a Users icon next to it. When migrating to shared Select, the icon was dropped because the Select API only accepts a string label. If icon-in-label is a desired pattern, the shared component APIs may need enhancement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Select component (src/components/ui/Select.tsx:15) has 'label?: string'. The original NotificationsPage had '<label><Users size={13} /> Recipient</label>'. After migration, just label='Recipient' with no icon.

summary: The feature implementation is solid and meets all requirements. EmptyState was adopted on ApprovalsPage, AuditTrailPage (both tabs), and ChangelogPage. ChangelogPage correctly replaces Loader2 spinner with Skeleton blocks. NotificationsPage uses shared Select and Input components. UsersPage and RolesPage were correctly not modified because their empty states are handled by the DataTable component's built-in emptyMessage prop (not inline JSX). Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) the Select label lost the Users icon since the shared Select API only supports string labels, and (2) the textarea remains native HTML since no shared Textarea component exists. Tests were properly updated to verify Skeleton loading and shared component usage. The worker followed procedure, all tests pass, and the code quality is clean.
